My granddaughter is three years old.
Her school is bilingual.
Our weekends are for the playground.
Clarinha's smile is the best part of my week.
Can you say that in English?
Very good! I am so proud of you.
Let's read this together.
What is this called in English?
Good job, sweetheart.
I speak English, and I am improving.
Good evening, Maria Clara. What a long day of sessions! Tell me something happy. Do you have family back home in Brazil?
I do! I have three children, and a granddaughter. Her name is Clarinha, and she is three years old. I am so proud of her.
Three years old! What is she like?
She is very curious. She asks about everything. And her school is bilingual, so she is learning English and Portuguese at the same time.
That is wonderful. So you encourage her at home too?
Every day. I support her, and we spend time together. On weekends, we go to the playground, and I say small things in English. And what about you? Do you have children?
I have two. They are older now. His name is Paolo, and her name is Sofia. Children grow up so fast, don't they?
They really do. That is why I am here, learning English. I want to speak it with Clarinha when she grows up. I am so proud to be her grandmother.
This is my granddaughter, Clarinha.
She is very curious, and I am so proud of her.

This is my granddaughter, Clarinha. She is three years old, and she is the light of my life. She is a very curious little girl. She asks about everything, all day long. She goes to a bilingual school, so she is learning English and Portuguese at the same time. On weekends, we spend time together. We go to the playground, we read books, and I try to say small things in English. I am learning too, so one day I can talk with her in both languages. I am so proud of her, and I will always support her.

Hello, Maria Clara. This is Clarinha's teacher at the bilingual school. I wanted to send you a short message about your granddaughter. Clarinha is doing very well. She is curious, she is kind, and she loves our English songs. Please continue to encourage her at home. When you spend time with her and say a few words in English, it helps her so much. Children grow up fast, and every small moment counts. You should be very proud. Have a wonderful week.
